meta.name: The Daily Reflection Tree

interpolation.answer: {NODE_ID.answer}     -> the option label the user chose at that node
interpolation.dominant: {axisN.dominant}     -> the pole with the highest tally on that axis (ties: 'balanced')

meta.notes: The tree is fully deterministic. No LLM is called at runtime. Question options carry signals that are tallied into per-axis pole counts; decision nodes route on accumulated signals or on prior answers.

START.text: Good evening. Take a breath. We're going to look at today together — not to grade it, just to notice it. Eight or nine questions. No right answers.

A1_OPEN.text: If you had to describe today in a single word, which fits best?

options.label: Productive
Mixed
Tough
Frustrating

A1_Q_HIGH.text: You called today "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When something went well, what made it happen?

options.label: I prepared the night before, and it paid off
I read the room and changed my approach mid-way
Someone on the team carried the moment
Honestly, it just worked out — lucky timing

A1_Q_LOW.text: You called today "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When things got hard, what was your first move?

options.label: I paused and asked: what's actually in my control here?
I named the problem out loud and asked for help
I waited to see if someone else would step in
I pushed through, head down, hoping it would pass

A1_Q2_INT.text: Think of one small choice today that no one would have noticed if you hadn't made it. Which one of these comes closest?

options.label: Saying yes to something I could easily have ducked
Saying no to a meeting or task that wasn't mine to carry
Speaking up when staying quiet would have been easier
Honestly, I'm not sure I made one

A1_Q2_EXT.text: Even on a hard day, there's usually one moment you steered. Where was yours today?

options.label: How I responded to a message I wanted to ignore
When I asked a question instead of assuming
When I chose to keep going instead of stopping
I don't think I steered anything today

A1_R_INT.text: You see your hand in what happened today. Not everything went your way — but you stayed in the driver's seat. That's agency. It's quiet, it doesn't announce itself, and most people don't notice when they have it. You did. Hold that.

A1_R_EXT.text: A hard day pulls our attention outward — to what others did, what the system didn't do, what we couldn't shape. That's fair, and it's honest. And still: somewhere in all of it, you made a call. You said it was "{A1_Q2_EXT.answer}". That's a small lever. Tomorrow has more of those moments than today did — they're just hard to see when you're tired.

A1_R_MIXED.text: Today held both — moments you steered and moments that steered you. That's most days, honestly. The skill isn't always being in control; it's noticing the difference between the two as it happens.

BRIDGE_1_2.text: We've looked at how you handled today. Now let's turn to something different — not what you did, but what you gave.

A2_OPEN.text: Pick one interaction from today that comes to mind right now. In that moment, were you mostly giving or mostly expecting?

options.label: I helped someone with something that wasn't mine to do
I taught or explained something, even informally
I was hoping for support that didn't come
I felt my work wasn't seen the way it deserved

in: I helped someone with something that wasn't mine to do
I taught or explained something, even informally
I was hoping for support that didn't come
I felt my work wasn't seen the way it deserved

A2_Q_GIVE.text: If no one would ever have known you did it — no credit, no thanks, no record — would you still have done it?

options.label: Yes, and I wouldn't think twice
Yes — it just needed doing
Probably, but I'd be quietly disappointed
Honestly, no — I wanted it noticed

A2_Q_EXPECT.text: Picture the recognition or support you wanted today. What specifically did you contribute that earned it?

options.label: A clear, real piece of work I can name
I held the team together in a way that's hard to point to
Effort and time, even if the output was small
I'm not sure — it was more about how hard the day felt

A2_Q2.text: Think of someone you didn't help today, but could have. What stopped you?

options.label: I noticed, and I made a deliberate choice not to
I did help — I'm drawing a blank on someone I missed
I was too deep in my own work to notice
I didn't notice anyone needed it

A2_R_CONTRIB.text: You gave something today that wasn't owed. That's the unglamorous, foundational kind of effort that quietly holds teams together. Most of it goes unrecorded. You'll know it mattered — even if no one writes it down.

A2_R_ENTITLE.text: There's a quiet voice in most of us that keeps a tally — what we gave, what we got, what we deserved. It's human. But when the tally runs the day, the day runs us. What if tomorrow, just for one stretch of it, you didn't keep score?

A2_R_MIXED.text: You gave, and you also wanted. Both real, both honest. The question worth carrying into tomorrow isn't which is right — it's which one you reached for first when you got tired.

BRIDGE_2_3.text: We've looked at how you showed up, and what you gave. One last shift — to the size of the world you were holding while you did it.

A3_OPEN.text: When you think about today's biggest challenge, who actually comes to mind first?

options.label: Just me — it was my problem to solve
My team — we were all in it
A specific colleague who had it harder than me
The customer or person we're ultimately serving

in: Just me — it was my problem to solve
My team — we were all in it
A specific colleague who had it harder than me
The customer or person we're ultimately serving

A3_Q_SELF.text: Zoom out for a second. If you replayed today and listed three people affected by your biggest challenge, who'd be on the list?

options.label: I can list them now — I just hadn't paused to
One or two teammates would feel it
The whole team plus people downstream
Honestly, I don't see others affected

A3_Q_OTHERS.text: Was there a moment today when you held someone else's situation in mind — not to fix it, just to understand it?

options.label: Yes, and I acted on it — small thing, but real
Yes, and I almost said something but didn't
Briefly, before I got pulled back to my own work
Honestly, no — I was in my own head

A3_Q2.text: Name one person you could thank, by name, tomorrow morning — and the specific thing you'd thank them for.

options.label: More than one — today gave me several
I have a name and a clear reason
I have a name, but I'd have to think about why
I'd have to think about both

A3_R_SELF.text: Today stayed close to you. That's not failure — sometimes the work demands it, and the day costs what it costs. But notice this: when the world shrinks to you-sized problems, the problems also start to feel you-sized. Tomorrow, look up once, on purpose. Just once.

A3_R_OTHERS.text: You weren't only inside your own day — you were reading the room. That's rarer than it sounds. Most people, by 7pm, have stopped noticing other people exist. You hadn't.

A3_R_TRANS.text: Today, the work was bigger than you. You held a customer, a teammate, a purpose in mind while you did it. That's where meaning quietly lives — in the radius of who you were working for, not just what you were working on.

SUMMARY.text:
Here's the shape of your day, in your own answers:

  • Agency (Locus):       you leaned **{axis1.dominant}**
  • Orientation:          you leaned **{axis2.dominant}**
  • Radius of concern:    you leaned **{axis3.dominant}**

You opened by calling today "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When you picked one interaction, it was: "{A2_OPEN.answer}". When you thought about the biggest challenge, the person on your mind was: "{A3_OPEN.answer}".

None of this is a grade. It's a mirror. The point of the mirror isn't to like or dislike what you see — it's to recognize it. Tomorrow is another sample.

END.text: That's it for tonight. Close the laptop. See you tomorrow.
